title: No-Power-Tools Spice Drawer Organizer
summary: A slanted spice-jar rack made from thin wood strips, sized entirely by test-fitting your own drawer and jars.
description:
This organizer has no fixed dimensions at all — every measurement comes from your own drawer and your own spice jars, checked by test-fitting as you go rather than by cutting to numbers from a plan.

Each frame is a simple rectangle of thin strips, but the **spacing between the two long strips is what tilts the jars to a readable angle**. Set that spacing by placing a jar on top of two trial strips and adjusting until the label reads clearly without the jar tipping over or the drawer failing to close — then cut the short connecting pieces to match that gap.

No power tools are required. A fine saw and miter box handle every cut here, which makes this a reasonable first hand-tool project.

tools.note: A fine saw and miter box, or a miter saw if you have one.

steps.title: Measure the drawer
steps.body: Measure the width of the drawer the organizer will live in.

steps.title: Set the tilt angle
steps.body: Cut two long strips to the drawer's width, set them in the drawer, and test-fit a spice jar on top of them. Adjust the spacing between the two strips until the jar tilts to a readable angle without blocking the drawer from closing.

steps.title: Cut the connecting pieces
steps.body: Measure the gap between the two long strips you just fitted, and cut two short connecting pieces to that exact length.

steps.title: Glue up a frame
steps.body: Sand all four pieces, then glue the short connecting pieces between the long strips to form a rectangular frame. Clamp until dry.

steps.title: Repeat to fill the drawer
steps.body: Repeat the process to build as many frames as needed to fill the width of the drawer.

steps.title: Add spacers and load the drawer
steps.body: Cut loose spacer strips to fill any leftover gap between the last frame and the drawer side — these don't need glue, just enough friction fit to keep everything from shifting. Load the drawer with spice jars.
